zh.Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England,: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾特拉福德 曼諾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Trafford於1423年出生在英國藍卡爾特拉福德 曼諾時，她的父親Edmund de Trafford I33歲，母親Alice de Venables25歲。她於大約1443年與Sir William Radcliffe在英國藍卡爾薩爾福德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1490年4月17日在英國艾可享年67歲，埋葬在英國藍卡爾薩爾福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Trafford于1423年出生在英国藍卡爾特拉福德 曼諾时，她的父亲Edmund de Trafford I33岁，母亲Alice de Venables25岁。她于大约1443年与Sir William Radcliffe在英国藍卡爾薩爾福德结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1490年4月17日在英国艾可享年67岁，埋葬在英国藍卡爾薩爾福德。

nameMeanings.name: Trafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Trafford (Lancashire), Trafford in Chipping Warden (Northamptonshire), and Bridge, Mickle, and Wimbolds Trafford (Cheshire). The Northamptonshire placename probably derives from Old English træppe ‘trap’ + ford ‘ford’. The Cheshire placenames derive from Old English trōg ‘trough’ + ford. The Lancashire placename is a development of Stretford (Lancashire); most cases of the surname probably derive from this place. Early members of the de Trafford family, recorded here from the 13th century, were also recorded in medieval documents as de Stratford(e). Both the surname and the placename lost the initial S-. The manor of Trafford was carved out of Stretford township, and perhaps because its manor house was located at some distance from Stretford village, Trafford was adopted for the new settlement from an early date. See also Stratford .

variants.name: Stratford
meanings: habitational name from any of various places called Stratford, for example in Bedfordshire, Buckinghamshire, Middlesex, Northamptonshire, Suffolk, Warwickshire, and Wiltshire, from Old English strǣt ‘Roman road’ (see Street ) + ford ‘ford’.

habitational name principally from Strafford Sands (now lost) in Mexborough (Yorkshire), presumed to be the ancient meeting-place of Strafforth Wapentake, and deriving from the same source as 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Trafford was born in 1423, in Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England, her father, Edmund de Trafford I, was 33 and her mother, Alice de Venables, was 25. She married Sir William Radcliffe about 1443, in Salford, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 17 April 1490, in Essex, England, at the age of 67, and was buried in Salford, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Trafford est née en 1423, à Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Edmund de Trafford I, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Alice de Venables, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Radcliffe environ 1443, à Salford, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 17 avril 1490, à Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterrée à Salford, Lancashire, Angleterre.

Sie hat um 1443 in Salford, Lancashire, England Sir William Radcliffe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb am 17. April 1490 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Essex, England und wurde in Salford, Lancashire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Traffordが1423年にイギリスランカシュアイアトラフォード マノーで生まれた時，父Edmund de Trafford Iは33歳，母Alice de Venablesは25歳であった。約1443年にイギリスランカシュアイアサルフォードでSir William Radcliffeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1490年4月17日にイギリスエセックスで67歳で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアサルフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Trafford(이)가 1423년 영국 랭카실르 트래퍼드 먼어에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund de Trafford I(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Venables(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 대략 1443년 영국 랭카실르 살퍼드에서 Sir William Radcliffe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1490년 4월 17일 영국 에세스에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 살퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Trafford nasceu em 1423, em Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund de Trafford I, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Venables, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William Radcliffe aproximadamente 1443, em Salford, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 17 de abril de 1490, em Essex, Inglaterra, com 67 anos, e foi sepultada em Salford, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Trafford родилась в 1423 г. в местности траффорд манор, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund de Trafford I было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Alice de Venables, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Radcliffe около 1443 г. в местности салфорд, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 17 апреля 1490 г. в местности эссекс, Англия в возрасте 67 лет и была похоронена в местности салфорд, ланкашир, Англия.
